paper_focus.title: CONCLUSIONS
paper_focus.research_objective: To determine the optimal compositions of Fe-Mn-Al alloys and the effect of heat treatment on their structure, mechanical, and physicochemical properties.
material_systems: Nominal composition ranges: Mn 5-45%, Al 2-14%, C 0.05-1.5%
Phases: γ-solid solution, α-solid solution, carbides, Mn-type brittle phase
main_process_variables: Melting: laboratory induction furnace, helium atmosphere, investment casting
Tensile samples: 5 mm diameter, 25 mm gauge length

visual_evidence.description: This figure presents the iron corner of the ternary Fe-Mn-Al phase diagram at 1150°C with approximately 1 wt% carbon, illustrating the phase boundaries between single-phase austenite and two-phase ferrite-austenite...
